title: Intense Brazilian Tapioca Casserole
description: A resinous, chewy meal-prep casserole that blends Brazilian tapioca with Korean-inspired namul seasoning, sour cream, and anti-inflammatory herbs for a hearty lunch.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add cooked black beans and namul-blanch-season to the skillet, stirring to combine and heat through.
In a bowl, whisk together tapioca flour, sour cream, and coconut milk until smooth.
Pour the flour mixture over the bean mixture, stirring to create a cohesive batter.
Transfer the batter to a greased baking dish, spreading evenly, then sprinkle diced onion and garlic on top for texture.
Bake in preheated oven for 18-20 minutes, or until the top is golden and the casserole sets.
Remove from oven, squeeze fresh lime juice over the top, and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Let cool 5 minutes before slicing; serve warm as a hearty lunch.
